title: Ministry moves to stabilise prices
desc: The Commerce Ministry plans to review the price control list to ensure it better reflects the current market, with the possibility of adding more essential items.

title: Grab, Bolt gear up for rising oil prices
desc: Online ride-hailing services have launched measures to deal with rising oil prices.

title: Thailand freezes diesel and controls prices as officials try to keep tourist spending stable in Pattaya
desc: Foreign and Thai tourists walk along Pattaya Beach Road as baht-buses and motorcycles pass by, reflecting the steady flow of visitors and local transport that could benefit from Thailand’s diesel price freeze and broader efforts to keep everyday travel and tourism costs stable. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – Thailand’s latest measures to stabilize energy prices and control the cost of essential goods may quietly help keep everyday tourist spending in Pattaya from rising, even as global energy markets remain volatile. title: British woman attempts self-harm in Pattaya hotel, rescued by police and rescue team
desc: Authorities check on the British woman, Ms. Ristona, lying on the bed in her Pattaya hotel room after a self-harm attempt. PATTAYA, Thailand – A 50-year-old British woman, identified as Ms. Ristona, was rushed to Pattaya Bhattamakun Hospital after attempting to harm herself in a hotel room on Soi 5, Pratumnak, March 17. Police from Pattaya City Station and members of the Sawang Boriboon Pattaya Rescue Foundation responded at 3:00 p.m. after hotel staff alerted authorities. title: Dark clouds and storms sweep Pattaya, weather to persist
desc: Dark clouds lingered over Pattaya Bay on Wednesday afternoon, signaling approaching thunderstorms. PATTAYA, Thailand – Residents and visitors in Pattaya were urged to stay alert as thunderstorms and strong winds swept through parts of the city on Wednesday. The Pattaya Municipal Enforcement had asked the public to help report local weather conditions to assist authorities in monitoring the situation. title: Pattaya ramps up Sukhumvit Road upgrade, 4-km recycling project to start after Songkran
desc: Mayor Poramet Ngampichet inspects the Sukhumvit Road section from Naklua to Central Pattaya ahead of a 4-kilometer road resurfacing project using recycling asphalt, aimed at improving traffic flow and safety for residents and tourists. PATTAYA, Thailand – Mayor Poramet Ngampichet led an on-site inspection of Sukhumvit Road’s second phase improvement project, covering the stretch from Naklua Intersection to Central Pattaya Intersection on March 18. title: Pattaya Mayor responds swiftly to damaged power pole complaint near railway road tunnel
desc: Mayor Poramet Ngampichet inspects the site near the railway road tunnel at Nong Ket Yai after residents reported a damaged power pole that had caused several traffic accidents, prompting swift repairs to improve public safety. PATTAYA, Thailand – Mayor Poramet Ngampichet led an urgent inspection on March 18 after residents reported a damaged power pole that had caused repeated accidents near the railway road tunnel at Nong Ket Yai intersection.
